what_it_does: This file is an orienteering ledger entry that documents the company config directory at /home/tim/.config/company, which holds the TLS certificate and private key used for serving the Company over Tailscale on workstation001.tail777bc2.ts.net.
observations: Holds a TLS cert/key pair under certs/ for workstation001.tail777bc2.ts.net
Cert subject CN matches the Tailscale serving host
Certificate validity is Jun 4 2026 to Sep 2 2026 GMT
Marked secrets: true because it contains a private key
standouts: Contains a live TLS private key in a user config directory
Certificate expires on 2026-09-02 and will need renewal
Explicitly flagged as secrets and not fully read
conventions: Follows the terrain-entry frontmatter schema with register, relation, kind, status, coverage, and tags
Uses wiki-style links for relates-to and data-store-for relationships
Uses an Evidence (Observed) section to separate inspected facts from inference
Marks sensitive material with secrets: true and avoids reading the private key
concerns: A private key is stored as plain files in ~/.config/company without described access controls
Certificate has a finite validity ending 2026-09-02 with no renewal plan shown
Status is unconfirmed, so the entry may not be fully verified
No git remote and serving host is hardcoded
notes: A secrets-bearing terrain entry that maps the Company TLS trust material to its Tailscale serving host and related systemd units.
questions: Which process exactly loads these certificate files and how?
What file permissions or encryption protect the private key?
How will the certificate be renewed before 2026-09-02?
Is the certificate issued by Tailscale or by another CA?
Why is the entry status marked unconfirmed?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is to record the Company config directory that holds TLS cert/key material for the Tailscale serving host, and the content does exactly that.
intention_signals: The entry appears to be trying to create an auditable, non-exposing record of the Company's TLS serving credentials so the serving topology can be understood and navigated safely.
novelty: It is a terrain entry that explicitly refuses to read a private key while still documenting the cert's subject, validity, and serving-host relationship, using secrets: true as a first-class signal.
contribution: Provides file-local metadata about the location, size, validity, and sensitivity of the Company TLS credentials, plus explicit links to the company serving layer and the systemd units that consume them, which a fused system would keep for navigation and operational awareness.
summary_for_embedding: Terrain ledger entry for the Company config directory at /home/tim/.config/company containing a TLS certificate and private key for workstation001.tail777bc2.ts.net used for Tailscale-served remote access.
intention_for_embedding: Document the location, validity, and sensitivity of the Company's TLS serving credentials and their connection to the serving systemd units.
